_meta.method: Compiled from English Wikipedia entries on the Trúc Lâm Zen tradition, Lâm Tế (Linji) heritage temples, and Plum Village's home temples in Huế. Coordinates from Wikipedia infoboxes or province centroids; flagged in notes when approximate.
_meta.coverageEstimate: Trúc Lâm head-monastery network mostly covered; major heritage Lâm Tế pagodas in Huế, Hanoi, and Saigon; Plum Village home temples. Many smaller pagodas and provincial Trúc Lâm sub-monasteries omitted.
knownGaps: Smaller Trúc Lâm thiền viện in less-prominent provinces
Hundreds of village-level Lâm Tế pagodas without standalone English-language sources
Most Quán Âm / Pure Land-leaning pagodas (out of scope: not primarily Zen practice)

places.name: Trúc Lâm Yên Tử Zen Monastery
places.lineage: Vietnamese Thiền (Trúc Lâm — historical founding site)
places.address: Yên Tử Mountain, Uông Bí, Quảng Ninh
places.notes: 13th-century founding seat of the Trúc Lâm school under King-monk Trần Nhân Tông; restored as an active monastery and major pilgrimage site on Yên Tử Mountain.

places.name: Trúc Lâm Tây Thiên Zen Monastery
places.address: Tây Thiên, Đại Đình, Tam Đảo, Vĩnh Phúc
places.notes: Major Trúc Lâm monastery in northern Vietnam, founded in the modern revival under Master Thích Thanh Từ.

places.name: Trúc Lâm Bạch Mã Zen Monastery
places.address: Bạch Mã National Park, Phú Lộc, Thừa Thiên-Huế
places.notes: Trúc Lâm monastery on Bạch Mã mountain. Coordinates approximate (Phú Lộc district).

places.name: Trúc Lâm Phượng Hoàng Zen Monastery
places.address: Phượng Hoàng, Nham Sơn, Yên Dũng, Bắc Giang
places.notes: Trúc Lâm monastery on Phượng Hoàng (Phoenix) mountain in northern Vietnam.

places.name: Trúc Lâm Phương Nam Zen Monastery
places.address: Mỹ Khánh, Phong Điền, Cần Thơ
places.notes: Largest Trúc Lâm monastery in the Mekong Delta; opened 2014.

places.name: Trúc Lâm Chánh Giác Zen Monastery
places.notes: Large Trúc Lâm monastery in the Mekong Delta region of southern Vietnam.

places.name: Trúc Lâm Sùng Phúc Zen Monastery
places.notes: Trúc Lâm monastery in the Long Biên district of Hanoi.

places.name: Trúc Lâm Hàm Rồng Zen Monastery
places.notes: Trúc Lâm monastery on Hàm Rồng (Dragon's Jaw) mountain in north-central Vietnam.

places.name: Trúc Lâm Tuệ Đức Zen Monastery
places.notes: Trúc Lâm monastery in Vĩnh Phúc province, northern Vietnam.

places.name: Diệu Trạm Monastery
places.lineage: Vietnamese Thiền (Plum Village — Thich Nhat Hanh tradition; sister to Từ Hiếu)
places.notes: Sister monastery of Từ Hiếu in Huế, home to Plum Village nuns and bhikshunis in the Thich Nhat Hanh tradition.

places.name: Thiên Mụ Pagoda
places.lineage: Vietnamese Thiền (Lâm Tế — historic patriarchate of central Vietnam)
places.notes: Founded 1601; the iconic seven-story pagoda overlooking the Perfume River. Long-standing seat of Lâm Tế lineage activity in Huế.

places.name: Bảo Quốc Pagoda
places.notes: Founded late 17th century by the Chinese Ch'an monk Giác Phong; historic Lâm Tế training temple in Huế.

places.name: Quán Sứ Pagoda
places.lineage: Vietnamese Thiền (Lâm Tế / Vietnamese Buddhist Sangha HQ)
places.notes: Headquarters of the Vietnam Buddhist Sangha; major active pagoda in central Hanoi.

places.name: Giác Lâm Pagoda
places.address: 565 Lạc Long Quân, Tân Bình, HCMC
places.notes: Founded 1744; one of the oldest Lâm Tế temples in southern Vietnam.

places.name: Phước Hải Pagoda
places.notes: Also known as Ngọc Hoàng (Jade Emperor) Pagoda; Lâm Tế-affiliated active temple in central HCMC.

places.name: Vĩnh Tràng Pagoda
places.address: 60 Nguyễn Trung Trực, Mỹ Tho, Tiền Giang
places.notes: Founded early 19th century; large Lâm Tế pagoda combining Vietnamese, Khmer, and European architecture.
